channel.title: Devleader.ca on .NET Ramblings
channel.description: Recent content in Devleader.ca on .NET Ramblings

item.title: Feature Slicing vs Clean Architecture in C#: Which One Should You Use?
item.description: Compare feature slicing vs clean architecture in C#. Learn the tradeoffs, when each approach fits best, and how to combine them in .NET projects. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/23/feature-slicing-vs-clean-architecture-in-c-which-one-should-you-use

item.title: When to Use State Pattern in C#: Decision Guide with Examples
item.description: Discover when to use state pattern in C# with real decision criteria, use case examples, and guidance on when simpler alternatives work better. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/23/when-to-use-state-pattern-in-c-decision-guide-with-examples

item.title: C# StringBuilder vs String Concatenation: Performance, Allocations, and Interpolated Handlers
item.description: Learn when to use C# StringBuilder vs string concatenation, how allocations differ, and how .NET 6 interpolated string handlers change performance calculations. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/22/c-stringbuilder-vs-string-concatenation-performance-allocations-and-interpolated-handlers

item.title: Command vs Strategy Pattern in C#: Key Differences Explained
item.description: Compare command vs strategy pattern in C# with side-by-side code examples, key behavioral differences, and guidance on when to use each. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/22/command-vs-strategy-pattern-in-c-key-differences-explained

item.title: Testing Feature Slices in C#: Unit Tests, Integration Tests, and What to Test
item.description: Learn how to test feature slices in C# effectively. Covers unit tests for handlers, integration tests with WebApplicationFactory, and when to use each testing strategy. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/21/testing-feature-slices-in-c-unit-tests-integration-tests-and-what-to-test

item.title: How to Implement State Pattern in C#: Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Learn how to implement state pattern in C# with a step-by-step guide covering state interfaces, concrete states, context classes, and transition logic. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/21/how-to-implement-state-pattern-in-c-stepbystep-guide

item.title: C# String to Byte Array: UTF-8, Encoding, and Span Conversions
item.description: Learn how to convert a C# string to byte array using UTF-8 encoding, Encoding class, Span, and .NET 7 u8 literals with performance-focused code examples. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/20/c-string-to-byte-array-utf8-encoding-and-span-conversions

item.title: Command Pattern Best Practices in C#: Code Organization and Maintainability
item.description: Master command pattern best practices in C# including single responsibility commands, proper undo implementation, command validation, and organized architectures. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/20/command-pattern-best-practices-in-c-code-organization-and-maintainability

item.title: The Context Switching Problem Every Dev Faces - Dev Leader Weekly 136
item.description: Welcome to another issue of Dev Leader Weekly! In this issue, I discuss context switching -- why it's unavoidable, how startups and big companies handle it differently, and what you can actually do about it. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/20/the-context-switching-problem-every-dev-faces-dev-leader-weekly-136

item.title: Feature Slicing Without MediatR in C#: Plain Handlers That Actually Work
item.description: Learn how to implement feature slicing in C# without MediatR. Build clean, testable feature handlers using plain classes and ASP.NET Core Minimal APIs. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/19/feature-slicing-without-mediatr-in-c-plain-handlers-that-actually-work

item.title: State Design Pattern in C#: Complete Guide with Examples
item.description: Master the state design pattern in C# with practical examples showing state transitions, behavior changes, and finite state machine implementations. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/19/state-design-pattern-in-c-complete-guide-with-examples

item.title: C# Multiline Strings: Verbatim Literals, Raw String Literals, and UTF-8 Literals
item.description: Learn every C# multiline string technique: verbatim @-strings, C# 11 raw string literals with triple quotes, and .NET 7 UTF-8 u8 literals with code examples. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/18/c-multiline-strings-verbatim-literals-raw-string-literals-and-utf8-literals

item.title: When to Use Command Pattern in C#: Decision Guide with Examples
item.description: Discover when to use command pattern in C# with real decision criteria, use case examples, and guidance on when simpler alternatives work better. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/18/when-to-use-command-pattern-in-c-decision-guide-with-examples

item.title: Feature Slice Folder Structure in .NET: Organizing a Real Project
item.description: Learn how to design a feature slice folder structure in .NET with real-world examples, structure decisions, and guidance on handling shared code and nested features. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/17/feature-slice-folder-structure-in-net-organizing-a-real-project

item.title: Adapter Pattern Real-World Example in C#: Complete Implementation
item.description: Build a complete adapter pattern real-world example in C# with a payment processing system featuring multiple provider adapters and unified interfaces. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/17/adapter-pattern-realworld-example-in-c-complete-implementation

item.title: C# String Searching: Contains, IndexOf, Split, Replace, and SearchValues
item.description: Learn C# string searching with Contains, IndexOf, Split, Replace, and .NET 8 SearchValues for SIMD-accelerated multi-character searching with code examples. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/16/c-string-searching-contains-indexof-split-replace-and-searchvalues

item.title: How to Implement Command Pattern in C#: Step-by-Step Guide
item.description: Learn how to implement command pattern in C# with a step-by-step guide covering command interfaces, concrete commands, invokers, and undo/redo support. Link to article: https://www.devleader.ca/2026/04/16/how-to-implement-command-pattern-in-c-stepbystep-guide
